title: Weighers, graders and sorters
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. Verify and maintain records on incoming and outgoing shipments involving inventory. Duties include verifying and recording incoming merchandise or material and arranging for the transportation of products. May prepare items for shipment. (Based on 49 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Weigh or measure materials, equipment, or products to maintain relevant records, using volume meters, scales, rules, or calipers. Collect or prepare measurement, weight, or identification labels and attach them to products. Record production data such as weights, types, quantities, and storage locations of materials.

Examine products or materials, parts, subassemblies, and packaging for damage, defects, or shortages, using specification sheets, gauges, and standards charts. Inspect materials, components, or products for surface defects and measure circuitry, using electronic test equipment, precision measuring instruments, microscope, and standard procedures.

Participate in the inspection, grading, sorting, storage, and post-harvest treatment of crops. Fit, alter, repair, and make made-to-measure clothing, according to customers' and clothing manufacturers' specifications and fit, and applying principles of garment design, construction, and styling. Identify articles' fabrics and original dyes by sight and touch, or by testing samples with fire or chemical reagents. Examine and sort into lots articles to be cleaned, according to color, fabric, dirt content, and cleaning technique required.

Signal or instruct other workers to weigh, move, or check products. Communicate with customers and vendors to exchange information regarding products, materials, and services. Inform farmers or farm managers of crop progress. Confer with customers to determine types of material and garment styles desired.

Sort products or materials into predetermined sequences or groupings for display, packing, shipping, or storage. Load, unload, or adjust materials or products on conveyors by hand, by using lifts, hoists, and scoops, or by opening gates, chutes, or hoppers. Collect product samples and prepare them for laboratory analysis or testing. Unload or unpack incoming shipments. Count or estimate quantities of materials, parts, or products received or shipped. Maintain inventory, ordering materials as required.

Clean, sterilize, and maintain equipment, machinery, and work stations, using hand tools, shovels, brooms, chemicals, hoses, and lubricants. Clean work areas, and maintain grounds and landscaping. Remove from stock products or loads not meeting quality standards, and notify supervisors or appropriate departments of discrepancies or shortages.

Drive trucks, following established routes, through residential streets or alleys or through business or industrial areas. Operate automated or semi-automated hoisting devices that raise refuse bins and dump contents into openings in truck bodies. Inspect trucks prior to beginning routes to ensure safe operating condition. Refuel trucks or add other fluids, such as oil or brake fluid.

Dump refuse or recyclable materials at disposal sites. Determine spotting procedures and proper solvents, based on fabric and stain types. Find cleaning techniques based on articles' fabrics, original dyes, and staining.

Tend spinning frames that draw out and twist roving or sliver into yarn. Compare product labels, tags, or tickets, shipping manifests, purchase orders, and bills of lading to verify accuracy of shipment contents, quality specifications, or weights. Count, sort, and weigh processed items. Adjust machine settings such as speed or tension to produce products that meet specifications.
Inspect products and examination records to determine the number of defects per worker and the reasons for examiners' rejections.
Place products in containers according to grade and mark grades on containers, while inspecting products to verify specifications and determining necessary machine adjustments.
Observe operations to detect defects, malfunctions, or supply shortages and notify supervisors or mechanics of any equipment malfunctions.
Weigh products or estimate their weight, visually or by feel, and discard inferior or defective products while placing acceptable products in containers for further processing.
Grade and sort products according to factors such as color, species, length, width, appearance, feel, smell, and quality to ensure proper processing and usage.
Tend machines that twist together multiple strands of yarn or insert additional twists into single strands of yarn to enhance strength and uniformity.
Clean and maintain supplies, tools, equipment, and storage areas to ensure compliance with safety regulations and inspect machinery to identify the need for repairs.
Document quantity, quality, type, weight, and test result data to maintain accurate shipping, receiving, and production records.
Record production data such as numbers and types of products processed and update logs accordingly.
Start machines, monitor their operation, and make adjustments as needed to ensure proper functioning.
Stock shelves, racks, and bins with new merchandise while examining and inspecting stock items for defects and reporting any damage to supervisors.
Receive, unload, and issue sales floor merchandise, ensuring proper inventory management by maintaining records of outgoing orders.
Transport materials, supplies, and finished products between storage and work areas, using equipment such as forklifts.
Dispose of damaged or defective items, return them to vendors when necessary, and recommend disposal of excess or obsolete stock.
Maintain compliance with safety regulations by keeping records on the use or damage of stock or stock-handling equipment.
Monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and adjust as necessary to maintain required conditions, making reports to supervisors when needed.
Adjust machine components and settings to regulate speeds, pressures, temperatures, and flow rates for optimal production outcomes.
Collect and inspect random samples during production runs to ensure completed products meet standards and specifications, while making adjustments if needed.
Perform inventory checks to identify items to be reordered or replenished, while examining merchandise to verify quantities and specifications.
Align and position materials being joined in processes to ensure accurate application of adhesives or other joining methods.
Transfer materials from storage areas and conduct inventory replenishment based on customer demand and advertised specials.
Review work orders, specifications, or instructions to determine materials, ingredients, procedures, and adjustments for production processes.
Select and install machine components, such as dies, molds, and cutters, according to specifications, using hand tools and measuring devices.
Record and maintain production and shipment data, including weights, quantities, types, and dimensions of materials or products produced and shipped.
Notify supervisors when products fail to meet standards or when production problems occur.
Synchronize speeds of sections of machines when producing products involving several steps or processes; activate machines to shape or form products.
Feed products into machines and clear jams, removing defective or substandard materials or products as necessary.
Monitor inventory levels regularly, ordering or requesting additional supplies as needed; requisition and store shipping materials and supplies to maintain inventory of stock.
Prepare documents, such as work orders, bills of lading, or shipping orders, and pack, seal, label, or affix postage on materials for shipping.
Deliver or route materials to departments using hand trucks, conveyors, or sorting bins and load or unload items from machines, conveyors, and transport vehicles.
Inspect materials and products for defects to ensure conformance to specifications; weigh or measure materials or products for compliance.
Transfer finished products, raw materials, tools, or equipment between storage and work areas, and remove products, machine attachments, or waste material from machines.
Stack or arrange tested products for further processing, shipping, or packaging and tie products in bundles for shipment according to prescribed procedures.
Maintain, clean, and lubricate machinery, equipment, and work areas for proper functioning and safe working conditions; perform minor repairs as needed.
Use hand or power tools to fasten, package, or stack materials and products, and apply seals or tags as necessary for identification.
Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to standards, and test products as required for quality assurance.
Monitor production operations or equipment to ensure specifications are conformed to and make necessary adjustments during the production process.
Identify and mark materials, products, and samples to follow instructions for production, shipment, and inventory control.
Signal and assist other workers in moving products during the production process and direct loading or unloading activities.
Inspect and approve packaging and labeling of products for compliance with specifications and regulations.
Record product testing results, including defect percentages and compliance with environmental standards, and report findings to supervisors.
Collaborate with coworkers on production schedules, sequences of deliveries, or special loading instructions as needed.

Record inspection or test data, such as weights, temperatures, grades, or moisture content, and quantities inspected or graded. Write test or inspection reports describing results, recommendations, or needed repairs. Inspect articles for defects and remove damaged or worn parts, using hand tools. Examine finished ware for defects and measure dimensions, using rule and thickness gauge. Verify accuracy of shapes and sizes of objects, using calipers and templates.

Mark items with details, such as grade or acceptance-rejection status. Measure dimensions of products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments, such as rulers, calipers, gauges, or micrometers. Compare colors, shapes, textures, or grades of products or materials with color charts, templates, or samples to verify conformance to standards. Assess training needs of staff and arrange for or provide appropriate instruction. Review customer requests for service to determine service needs and deploy appropriate resources to provide service. Maintain logs of recycling materials received or shipped to processing companies. Prepare and maintain work records and reports of information such as employee time and wages, daily receipts, or inspection results.

Collect or select samples for testing or for use as models. Prepare items for shipment. Conduct staff meetings to relay general information or to address specific topics, such as safety. Train new employees in areas such as safety procedures or equipment operation.

Inspect equipment for wear and for conformance to specifications. Verify tank car, barge, or truck load numbers to ensure car placement accuracy based on written or verbal instructions. Start pumps and adjust valves or cables to regulate the flow of products to vessels, using knowledge of loading procedures.

Align and stitch or glue materials such as fabric, fleece, leather, or wood, to join parts. Construct, decorate, or repair leather products according to specifications, using sewing machines, needles and thread, leather lacing, glue, clamps, hand tools, or rivets. Dye, soak, polish, paint, stamp, stitch, stain, buff, or engrave leather or other materials to obtain desired effects, decorations, or shapes. Clean and polish shoes. Oversee recycling pick-up or drop-off programs to ensure compliance with community ordinances. Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as promotions, transfers, or disciplinary measures. Resolve personnel problems, complaints, or formal grievances when possible, or refer them to higher-level supervisors for resolution.

Perform the same work duties as those supervised, or perform more difficult or skilled tasks or assist in their performance. Collaborate with workers and managers to solve work-related problems. Plan work schedules and assign duties to maintain adequate staff for effective performance of activities and response to fluctuating workloads. Evaluate employee performance and prepare performance appraisals.

Estimate material, time, and staffing requirements for a given project, based on work orders, job specifications, and experience. Record operating data such as products and quantities pumped, gauge readings, and operating times, manually or using computers. Monitor product movement to and from storage tanks, coordinating activities with other workers to ensure constant product flow.

Observe positions of cars passing loading spouts, and swing spouts into the correct positions at the appropriate times. Examine freight to determine loading sequences. Coordinate shipments of recycling materials with shipping brokers or processing companies.

Cut out parts, following patterns or outlines, using knives, shears, scissors, or machine presses. Smooth surfaces of finished pieces, using rubber scrapers and wet sponges. Raise and shape clay into wares, such as vases and pitchers, on revolving wheels, using hands, fingers, and thumbs. Prepare work for sale or exhibition, and maintain relationships with retail, pottery, art, and resource networks that can facilitate sale or exhibition of work.

Adjust wheel speeds according to the feel of the clay as pieces enlarge and walls become thinner. Clean oxides and scales from parts or fittings, using steam sprays or chemical and water baths. Heat billets, bars, plates, rods, and other stock to specified temperatures preparatory to forging, rolling, or processing, using oil, gas, or electrical furnaces. Inspect physical condition of recycling or hazardous waste facility for compliance with safety, quality, and service standards. Schedule times of shipment and modes of transportation for materials. Provide training to recycling technicians or community service workers on topics such as safety, solid waste processing, or general recycling operations. Design community solid and hazardous waste management programs.

Verify dimensions of products, using measuring instruments, such as calipers, vernier gauges, or protractors. Remove defects, such as chips, burrs, or lap corroded or pitted surfaces. Prepare inserts, heel pads, and lifts from casts of customers' feet.

Teach pottery classes. Create or manage recycling operations budgets. Inspect job sites to determine the extent of maintenance or repairs needed. Make presentations to educate the public on how to recycle or on the environmental advantages of recycling. Inspect physical condition of recycling or hazardous waste facility for compliance with safety, quality, and service standards. Investigate violations of solid waste or recycling ordinances. Develop community or corporate recycling plans and goals to minimize waste and conform to resource constraints. Implement grant-funded projects, monitoring and reporting progress in accordance with sponsoring agency requirements.

Perform general warehouse activities, such as opening containers and crates, filling warehouse orders, assisting in taking inventory, and weighing and checking materials. Schedule movement of recycling materials into and out of storage areas. Make, modify, and repair orthopedic or therapeutic footwear according to doctors' prescriptions, or modify existing footwear for people with foot problems and special needs. Clean interiors of tank cars or tank trucks, using mechanical spray nozzles. Examine parts to ensure metal shades and colors conform to specifications, using knowledge of metal heat-treating.
Tap or tilt molds to ensure uniform distribution of materials, and clean, finish, and lubricate molds and mold parts.
Construct or form molds for use in casting clay or plaster objects, and attach handles to pottery pieces.
Pour, pack, spread, or press plaster, concrete, or other materials into or around models or molds.

Carve designs or figures in full or bas relief on stone, employing knowledge of stone carving techniques and sense of artistry, and shape, trim, or touch up roughed-out designs with appropriate tools to finish carvings.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces and mix and apply glazes to pottery pieces using tools like spray guns.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale, and pack products for shipping.
Design spaces to display pottery for sale and develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects.
Operate pug mills to blend and extrude clay, and oversee the process of shaping, bending, or joining sections of glass.
Measure and cut products to specified dimensions, and adjust pressures, temperatures, and trimming tool settings as required.
Verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications and read work orders or examine parts to determine parts or sections of products to be produced.

Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications, using instruments such as micrometers, calipers, magnifiers, or rulers, and record manufacturing information, such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced.
Inspect equipment to detect wear and damage and perform minor repairs, adjustments, or maintenance tasks, such as oiling parts.
Provide information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations, and keep informed of shut-down and emergency evacuation procedures.

Sort metals to separate high-grade metals, such as copper, brass, and aluminum, for recycling; operate balers to compress recyclable materials into bundles or bales; and collect and sort recyclable construction materials.

Monitor machine cycles and mill operation to detect jamming and to ensure that products conform to specifications, while adjusting and correcting machine set-ups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate product defects.
Set specifications for materials, dimensions, and finishes, and confer with customers to assess customer needs or obtain feedback as part of the crafting process.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand, using a variety of methods and materials, and cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or otherwise process materials using hand tools, power tools, or machinery.
Fabricate patterns or templates to guide craft production, and sketch or draw objects to be crafted.
Sell tickets and collect fees from customers in the context of recreational activities, and direct patrons to rides, seats, or attractions as part of the facility operations.
Record mill production on schedule sheets.

Weigh and mix pelletized, granular, or powdered thermoplastic materials and coloring pigments; Load machine hoppers with mixed materials, using augers, or stuff rolls of plastic dough into machine cylinders; Operate processing equipment, such as fiber-sorters and grinders, to sort, crush, or grind recyclable materials.
Order tooling for jobs.

Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines; Clean machines, tooling, or parts, using solvents or solutions and rags; Maintain machines and remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, using hand tools; Control coolant systems.

Transfer commands from servers to computer numerical control (CNC) modules, using computer network links; Adjust machine feed and speed, change cutting tools, or adjust machine controls when automatic programming is faulty or if machines malfunction; Stop machines to remove finished workpieces or to change tooling, setup, or workpiece placement, according to required machining sequences; Modify cutting programs to account for problems encountered during operation, and save modified programs; Insert control instructions into machine control units to start operation; Implement changes to machine programs, and enter new specifications, using computers; Enter commands or load control media, such as tapes, cards, or disks, into machine controllers to retrieve programmed instructions.

Check to ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation; Monitor machine operation and control panel displays, and compare readings to specifications to detect malfunctions; Listen to machines during operation to detect sounds such as those made by dull cutting tools or excessive vibration, and adjust machines to compensate for problems; Review program specifications or blueprints to determine and set machine operations and sequencing, finished workpiece dimensions, or numerical control sequences; Confer with supervisors or programmers to resolve machine malfunctions or production errors or to obtain approval to continue production.

Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform one or more machine functions on metal or plastic workpieces; Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments, templates, and fixtures; Determine the sequence of machine operations, and select the proper cutting tools needed to machine workpieces into the desired shapes; Revise programs or tapes to eliminate errors, and retest programs to check that problems have been solved; Analyze job orders, drawings, blueprints, specifications, printed circuit board pattern films, and design data to calculate dimensions, tool selection, machine speeds, and feed rates; Compare encoded tapes or computer printouts with original part specifications and blueprints to verify accuracy of instructions; Observe machines on trial runs or conduct computer simulations to ensure that programs and machinery will function properly and produce items that meet specifications.

Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents; Examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements; Review orders, production schedules, blueprints, or shipping or receiving notices to determine work sequences and material shipping dates, types, volumes, or destinations; Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials; Maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities; Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.

Recommend or implement personnel actions, such as employee selection, evaluation, rewards, or disciplinary actions; Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers; Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems.

Enforce safety rules and regulations; Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations or production goals; Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation, equipment performance, work methods, or customer services; Resolve worker problems or collaborate with employees to assist in problem resolution; Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers.

Sort shop orders into groups to maximize materials utilization and minimize machine setup time; Prepare geometric layouts from graphic displays, using computer-assisted drafting software or drafting instruments and graph paper; Write simple programs for computer-controlled machine tools; Input initial part dimensions into machine control panels; Lay out and mark areas of parts to be shot peened and fill hoppers with shot; Write instruction sheets and cutter lists for a machine's controller to guide setup and encode numerical control tapes; Align and secure pattern film on reference tables of optical programmers, and observe enlarger scope views of printed circuit boards; Modify existing programs to enhance efficiency; Determine reference points, machine cutting paths, or hole locations, and compute angular and linear dimensions, radii, and curvatures; Remove and replace dull cutting tools; Mount, install, align, and secure tools, attachments, fixtures, and workpieces on machines, using hand tools and precision measuring instruments; Enter coordinates of hole locations into program memories by depressing pedals or buttons of programmers.
Inspect materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications.
